steps.title: Use a table value to test the equations
steps.content: Each row $(x, y)$ from the table must satisfy the correct equation. Plug a row into each candidate; only one will produce a true statement for every row.

steps.title: Verify the form
steps.content:
Substituting the given table values into $3x + sy = 11s$ produces a consistent identity in $s$ for every row, while the other three equations fail at least one row.

For instance, the row $(x, y) = \left(\dfrac{11s}{3},\, 0\right)$ satisfies $3 \cdot \dfrac{11s}{3} + s \cdot 0 = 11s$ — true. The other choices' constants ($11$ vs. $11s$) or coefficient swaps don't survive the substitution.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
